---
title: "Hongming Guo"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero-banner}

# Hongming Guo

::: {.profile-photo}
![](images/ghm.jpg)
:::

Economics Graduate Student

I am a graduate student in economics with research interests in labor economics and consumption economics. My current work focuses on gender and labor market outcomes, as well as the effects of automation and service-sector transformation on household welfare.

[About](about.qmd){.btn-academic} [Research](research.qmd){.btn-academic} [CV](CV.md){.btn-academic}

:::

## Research Interests

- Labor Economics
- Consumption Economics

## Current Research

- Gender and labor market
- Automation, service-sector transformation, and household welfare

## Brief Bio

I am interested in empirical research on how labor market change shapes household decision-making and well-being. My work is motivated by questions at the intersection of labor, structural transformation, and welfare, with an emphasis on careful measurement and policy-relevant interpretation.
